title: Finding David

jokes.text: You had a room full of brown bags back home, which brown bag?!
jokes.setup: Moira frantically looking for her missing brown bag
jokes.punchline: Johnny's exasperated reminder of their former wealth and excess
jokes.explanation: Good fish-out-of-water humor contrasting their past excess with current desperation

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Has anyone seen my brown bag? My favorite brown bag?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Moira, you have seventeen brown bags. You have brown bags in colors that brown hasn't even been invented yet.

jokes.text: That's an ostrich bag.
jokes.setup: Moira desperately describing her 'crocodile bag'
jokes.punchline: Johnny's matter-of-fact correction about exotic leather types
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery showcasing the family's ridiculous former lifestyle and Johnny's practical nature

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It's a crocodile bag. A real crocodile bag.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: It's not a crocodile bag, Moira. It's an alligator bag.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, what's the difference?

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: One is a crocodile and one is an alligator.

jokes.text: David must've taken it, along with my last molecule of hope!
jokes.setup: Moira's dramatic search for her bag
jokes.punchline: Overly dramatic declaration linking material loss to existential despair
jokes.explanation: Classic Moira melodrama with theatrical language perfectly fitting her character

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: My bag! My beautiful, Italian leather bag! Gone. Just like my dignity, my fortune, and my will to live.

jokes.text: I bet he's pawned it by now, to pay for manicures.
jokes.setup: Discussion of David potentially stealing the bag
jokes.punchline: Moira's assumption about what David would spend money on
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about David's grooming habits from his mother's perspective

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David would never steal. He doesn't have the physical dexterity for it.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Besides, if he had that kind of money, he'd spend it on something practical. Like a black turtleneck. Or another black turtleneck.

jokes.text: Mrs. Rose, you got out of bed. Humph!
jokes.setup: Stevie's casual observation about Moira getting dressed
jokes.punchline: Moira's indignant reaction to the implication she's been bedridden
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective clash between Stevie's directness and Moira's dignity

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: You've been in that robe all day.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I have not been in this robe all day. I've been in several robes.

jokes.text: That's, very considerate, because I know you and David had a... Intimate uh, relationship.
jokes.setup: Johnny trying to be polite to Stevie about helping find David
jokes.punchline: Awkward acknowledgment of David and Stevie's sexual relationship
jokes.explanation: Cringe humor from Johnny's discomfort discussing his son's sex life

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Stevie, I really appreciate you helping us look for David. I know you two are... close.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: Yeah, well, he's important to me.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Yes, I'm aware. Very... intimately aware.

jokes.text: Can you ask her if she has any extra bedspreads?
jokes.setup: Family discussing David's disappearance with Stevie
jokes.punchline: Alexis interrupts crisis with mundane domestic request
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's self-absorption and poor timing, good character moment

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Has anyone seen David? He's been gone for like three hours and he didn't tell me where he was going.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David is a grown man, Alexis. He doesn't need to report his whereabouts to you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, but he always tells me. Stevie, you haven't heard from him?

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: No, not since this morning.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I'm sure he's fine. He probably just needed some air.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Okay, but like, has anyone seen my good hair clip? Because I can't find it anywhere.

jokes.text: I'm still here!
jokes.setup: Johnny asking if someone can ask Stevie about bedspreads
jokes.punchline: Moira's dramatic announcement of her presence
jokes.explanation: Moira's theatrical tendency to announce herself dramatically even in mundane situations

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I'm here!

jokes.text: Like, Ted keeps harassing me for an answer to the whole, am I gonna marry him thing
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining why she needs more space
jokes.punchline: Describing a marriage proposal as 'harassment'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis perspective - viewing romantic commitment as an inconvenience

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I need more space in this relationship.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: More space? Alexis, I proposed to you.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I know, and that was like, harassment.

jokes.text: Well, I'm-I'm sorry for your loss.
jokes.setup: Johnny's outburst about speaking respectfully to Stevie
jokes.punchline: Stevie treating his anger like a death
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan response that's perfectly economical and character-specific for Stevie's dry wit

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I'm so sorry for your loss.

jokes.text: when he'd charter the jet without permission?
jokes.setup: Discussing David's history of running away
jokes.punchline: Their idea of teenage rebellion involved private jets
jokes.explanation: Great fish-out-of-water humor contrasting normal teenage rebellion with wealthy privilege

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, David's idea of teenage rebellion involved private jets.

jokes.text: If there's one thing David knows, it's the street value of a woman's bag.
jokes.setup: Moira worrying about David having no money
jokes.punchline: Moira's confidence in David's ability to fence luxury goods
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny maternal confidence in her son's criminal potential

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: David will be fine. He has an eye for luxury goods. I'm sure he'll find a way to... fence them.

jokes.text: my great grandmother took it from her husband when she left him, and it has been passed down through all the women in my family, as emergency currency, in case we need to leave our husbands in the middle of the night.
jokes.setup: Explaining the bag's sentimental value
jokes.punchline: Family heirloom specifically for escaping marriages
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly dark family tradition that's both absurd and oddly empowering

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: This bag is a family heirloom. It's been passed down through generations of Rose women, each one using it for the exact same purpose.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What purpose?

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Escaping marriages.

jokes.text: But you know Alexis will! Yeah, more than once, probably.
jokes.setup: Moira saying she'll never need the escape bag
jokes.punchline: Both parents immediately agreeing Alexis will need it multiple times
jokes.explanation: Perfect parental consensus about their daughter's relationship patterns

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I will never need an escape bag.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: You're right, you won't.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Alexis, however...

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Oh, she'll need multiple.

jokes.text: Actually, you're standing in my living room, you let yourself in.
jokes.setup: Mutt saying he shouldn't come in
jokes.punchline: Pointing out he's already inside uninvited
jokes.explanation: Good deadpan observation highlighting the absurdity of the situation

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I shouldn't come in.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I'm already inside uninvited.

jokes.text: I would love to feel worse about your parents not selling the town, but...
jokes.setup: Alexis explaining family complications
jokes.punchline: Mutt's inability to muster sympathy for their wealth problems
jokes.explanation: Perfect class commentary delivered with Mutt's characteristic dry detachment

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: My family is going through some really complicated stuff right now.

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Yeah, I heard. That's rough.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, we lost everything. Our money, our house, our staff...

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Mm-hmm.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So, I was wondering if maybe you could—

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I'm not gonna be able to help you with that.

jokes.text: it's so tragic, 'cause my mom is in this like, waking coma, and my dad has developed more of a stutter
jokes.setup: Alexis explains the family situation
jokes.punchline: Dramatic overstatement of her parents' minor issues
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alexis's tendency to dramatize

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: My parents have completely lost control of their lives. My mother hasn't had a facial in weeks, and my father's been wearing the same Tom Ford suit two days in a row.

jokes.text: By who? By me.
jokes.setup: Mutt asking who misinformed Ted about their relationship status
jokes.punchline: Alexis immediately admitting she was the source
jokes.explanation: Perfectly economical admission of guilt with great timing

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: Who told you that we were together?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I did.

jokes.text: Thanks, I make sure to moisturize.
jokes.setup: Alexis commenting on his soft hands despite manual labor
jokes.punchline: Mutt's matter-of-fact response about skincare
jokes.explanation: Unexpected vanity from the seemingly rugged character

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Your hands are so soft! How is that possible when you do all this manual labor?

dialogue.speaker: Mutt
dialogue.line: I use a lot of moisturizer.

jokes.text: What, are you here to tell me Alexis has skipped town with my lawn mower?!
jokes.setup: Roland seeing the Rose family at his door
jokes.punchline: Assuming they're there about more stolen property
jokes.explanation: Establishes pattern of the Rose family 'borrowing' things from locals

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Oh no, what did you steal now?

jokes.text: Roland, these people have literally hit rock bottom, they're not in the mood for your jokes right now. She is.
jokes.setup: Jocelyn defending the Rose family's dignity
jokes.punchline: Roland's immediate contradiction about Moira
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamic showing Roland's inability to resist teasing Moira

dialogue.speaker: Jocelyn
dialogue.line: The Rose family has real dignity and class.

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Have you seen Moira in those wigs?

jokes.text: Oh, I haven't lost him, i know exactly where he is. He's enjoying happy hour at the viceroy without me.
jokes.setup: Roland expressing sympathy about losing a child
jokes.punchline: Moira's bitter certainty about David abandoning her for luxury
jokes.explanation: Moira's dramatic interpretation of abandonment in luxury hotel terms

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh, he'll abandon me the moment he finds a cashmere sweater and a boy who knows how to make a cocktail.

jokes.text: statistics tell us you have a 48 hour window before you can assume somebody is dead. Yeah, I just said it's been three days. Then do we call off the search?
jokes.setup: Roland sharing morbid statistics about missing persons
jokes.punchline: Roland's logical conclusion about calling off the search
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation of Roland's tactless logic about missing persons

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: You know, statistically speaking, most missing persons are found within the first 48 hours. After that, the odds drop significantly. So really, if we're being practical about it, we should probably call off the search.

jokes.text: Well, good news, folks! They found the truck. And David. Well, that didn't come up.
jokes.setup: Roland announcing good news about finding the truck and David
jokes.punchline: Revealing he didn't actually confirm David was with the truck
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection humor playing on assumptions and Roland's casual approach to important details

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I found the truck! And David's with it!

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: That's wonderful!

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, I found the truck.

jokes.text: Well, I think they would've told me if there's a dead body with the truck, honey.
jokes.setup: Family panicking about David's status
jokes.punchline: Roland's casual logic about corpse notification protocols
jokes.explanation: Roland's characteristic tactlessness about morbid possibilities

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: Well, if David was dead, somebody would've called us by now. That's how it works.

jokes.text: My best friend in kindergarten ran away, and her parents didn't notice, and she ended up getting adopted by this really rich Asian family in Vancouver, but then they had their own kid, so she kind of got neglected a bit, and I can't remember whether she's back on the streets now, or...
jokes.setup: Alexis trying to contribute to conversation about missing people
jokes.punchline: Increasingly dark and convoluted story delivered casually
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis ramble - starts innocent but becomes increasingly disturbing while maintaining her oblivious tone

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my god, I actually know about this. So my friend Mutt—well, not my friend Mutt, just this guy Mutt—anyway, he went missing for like three days, and it turned out he was just at a cabin. But like, a really weird cabin. And the guy who owned it? Total psychopath. He had like, teeth everywhere. Human teeth. And he kept them in jars. And one time he tried to—actually, you know what, I'm not gonna finish that story because it gets really dark, but basically he's in jail now, which is probably for the best.

jokes.text: You just ordered the parfait.
jokes.setup: Alexis claiming she hasn't been eating due to stress
jokes.punchline: Ted's immediate contradiction with evidence
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective calling out of Alexis's dramatic exaggeration

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: You haven't been eating? Alexis, you just finished a whole rotisserie chicken in my office.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: That was stress eating.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: You stress ate an entire chicken?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I stress eat.

jokes.text: Oh, no, that guy was like, a known alcoholic, and they pulled him out a week ago.
jokes.setup: Alexis saying she pictures David at the bottom of a lake
jokes.punchline: Ted's matter-of-fact correction about a different body
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny veterinarian matter-of-factness about local deaths

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I picture you at the bottom of a lake.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Why would you picture me at the bottom of a lake?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I don't know, it's just the first image that comes to mind.

dialogue.speaker: Ted
dialogue.line: Well, technically, it would be a quarry.

jokes.text: Technically though, someone did leave, and so the dust just hasn't really settled on the whole... us leaving thing. - Yet.
jokes.setup: Alexis explains why she can't commit to Ted
jokes.punchline: Circular logic using David's departure to avoid marriage decision
jokes.explanation: Shows Alexis's talent for avoiding commitment through convoluted reasoning

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I can't marry Ted because David left town, and if David can just leave whenever he wants, then what's stopping Ted from leaving me?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I didn't leave town.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You literally just said you were moving to California.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: That's not leaving town, that's moving away for a new life opportunity.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Exactly. So Ted could do the same thing.

jokes.text: now don't take this the wrong way, but, is this car your home?
jokes.setup: Moira observing Stevie's messy car
jokes.punchline: Moira's tactless but genuine question about Stevie's living situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira - trying to be polite while being incredibly rude

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Darling, is this car a residence, or are you just transporting a residence?

jokes.text: I'm just super tired from walking, though.
jokes.setup: Being told they found David and need to go get him
jokes.punchline: Alexis's excuse for not wanting to help rescue her brother
jokes.explanation: Classic Alexis self-centeredness - minimal effort is exhausting

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I would, but I'm getting a manicure and I already paid for it.

jokes.text: Do I have to remind you of the time that I was taken hostage on David geffen's yacht by Somali pirates for a week, and nobody answered my texts?!
jokes.setup: Family being dramatic about David's disappearance
jokes.punchline: Alexis's casual mention of being kidnapped by pirates
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Alexis's absurdly dramatic past delivered as casual complaint about family neglect

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I mean, I've been kidnapped by pirates before, and I didn't make this big of a fuss about it.

jokes.text: I had just had my eyelashes dyed, everything was cloudy!
jokes.setup: Explaining why they didn't respond to Alexis's kidnapping texts
jokes.punchline: Moira's beauty treatment excuse for missing emergency
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation of the absurdity - cosmetic procedures taking priority over daughter's kidnapping

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I was in the middle of my microdermabrasion. I couldn't possibly interrupt that. One doesn't just stop mid-dermis.

jokes.text: We slept together! Ooh...
jokes.setup: Alexis telling David about the Mutt situation getting complicated
jokes.punchline: David's disgusted reaction to learning about his sister and his ex
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment from sibling oversharing about sex

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: So the thing with Mutt is getting kind of complicated.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What do you mean complicated?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Well, we're kind of seeing each other.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're seeing Mutt? Mutt Schitt?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Yeah, but also kind of Ted.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: You're seeing Ted and Mutt at the same time?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Not at the same time, just... consecutively.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Alexis, that's not better. That's actually worse.

jokes.text: There's a bug on your dress. - Ew! Ugh! Oh!
jokes.setup: David deflects serious conversation
jokes.punchline: Alexis's dramatic reaction to bug, physical comedy ensues
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy and character moment

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I don't really want to talk about this right now.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is that a bug?!

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? No, that's—

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Get it off! Get it off me!

jokes.text: Miriam makes the most delicious butter. Yeah, I mean, she starts pounding that cream about a half hour too early in the morning, but it tastes exactly like the butter we had at the Ritz in Paris
jokes.setup: David talking about his time with the Amish
jokes.punchline: Complaining about farm chores while comparing to luxury hotel
jokes.explanation: Perfect David - finding ways to complain about free housing while referencing luxury experiences

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I had to wake up at 4 AM to milk cows. Do you know what I was doing at 4 AM before that? Sleeping. In a bed. A very nice bed. With Egyptian cotton sheets.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And then I had to shovel manure. Actual manure. While wearing a scratchy wool shirt that made me look like I was auditioning for a depression-era documentary.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: The closest I'd come to manual labor before that was pointing at things I wanted in a catalog.

jokes.text: We've spent two sleepless nights, wondering what would happen if nobody came. Would we be stuck with him?!
jokes.setup: Amish man being polite about David's stay
jokes.punchline: Blunt admission of relief that someone came to get David
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery from typically reserved Amish character about David being insufferable

dialogue.speaker: Amish man
dialogue.line: Well, it's been very nice having you stay with us. You've been a wonderful guest.

dialogue.speaker: Amish man
dialogue.line: But I have to say, I am so relieved that someone finally came to pick you up.

jokes.text: Restraint.
jokes.setup: Listing virtues David's stay taught them
jokes.punchline: The final 'virtue' delivered with obvious exhaustion
jokes.explanation: Perfect single word punchline delivered with maximum economy and impact

dialogue.speaker: David Rose
dialogue.line: We learned humility, we learned to appreciate the simple things, and we learned that apparently Amish people can go to Costco.

jokes.text: And yet your time with us has come to an end.
jokes.setup: David saying he's not ready to leave
jokes.punchline: Amish man's polite but firm dismissal
jokes.explanation: Politely firm dismissal disguised as philosophy - perfect Amish diplomacy

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm not ready to leave.

dialogue.speaker: Amish man
dialogue.line: Well, you have to. Your time is up.

jokes.text: These three days you spent with us David, will remain in our memories for many days, and many, many, many nights.
jokes.setup: Giving a farewell speech to David
jokes.punchline: Emphasis on how the memory will haunt them
jokes.explanation: Subtle escalation suggesting David's visit was traumatic rather than pleasant

dialogue.speaker: Amish man
dialogue.line: David, we will cherish the memory of you... and it will haunt us. Forever.

jokes.text: I imagine it's customary for strangers to partake in a good, old fashioned amish lunch? Not always.
jokes.setup: Johnny hoping for Amish hospitality
jokes.punchline: Amish man's quick rejection of free meal
jokes.explanation: Simple rejection humor - even traditional hospitality has limits with this family

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: I was hoping you might offer us some traditional Amish hospitality. Perhaps a meal?

dialogue.speaker: Amish man
dialogue.line: No.

jokes.text: I didn't go missing, David. The FBI knew where I was the entire time.
jokes.setup: David complaining nobody freaked out when Alexis went missing
jokes.punchline: Alexis casually revealing federal surveillance
jokes.explanation: Brilliant casual revelation of Alexis's criminal connections and government tracking

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Nobody even cared that Alexis was missing. I mean, shouldn't someone have noticed? Shouldn't someone have called the police?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Actually, the FBI called me. Apparently I'm on some kind of federal watchlist.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: What? Why would you be on a federal watchlist?

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: I don't know, something about that time I accidentally brought a prohibited substance through customs? Or maybe it was the other time. Anyway, they check in on me regularly.

jokes.text: You told me that Diane Sawyer gave you that bag, and that it was fake.
jokes.setup: David explaining why he took the bag
jokes.punchline: Revealing Moira's lie about the bag's provenance
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Moira's tendency to embellish stories

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I took the bag because Moira said it was a limited edition Birkin from a personal friend in Paris, but it's clearly a knockoff from a street vendor in Chinatown.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: It is NOT a knockoff. It's a homage.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It has a typo. It says 'Birkin' with two R's.

jokes.text: I don't know David, why did you steal my eyeliner? - That was a phase in high school!
jokes.setup: Alexis brings up past theft
jokes.punchline: David's defensive explanation about experimenting with makeup
jokes.explanation: Good sibling dynamic and character backstory

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You stole my makeup in high school.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I didn't steal it. I was experimenting.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: You were experimenting with my MAC lipstick?

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: It was a very good shade. I was exploring my identity.

jokes.text: Okay, because I have told a lot of people that Diane Sawyer sells fake bags on the down-low.
jokes.setup: Being confronted about lying about the bag
jokes.punchline: Moira's concern about spreading celebrity gossip
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moira priority - worried about defaming a celebrity rather than family drama

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: I didn't lie about the bag. I simply didn't tell the truth. There's a difference.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: And before you start with me about spreading celebrity gossip, that bag was a gift from a very famous person, and I would never betray their confidence by telling you who it was.

jokes.text: I hitchhiked, in the middle of the night, wearing all black! I was basically asking to get hit by a car!
jokes.setup: David explains his dramatic exit
jokes.punchline: Realizes his own poor planning choices
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware moment showing David's dramatic but impractical nature

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I orchestrated a dramatic exit, sweeping out of the room with purpose and intention, only to realize I'd left my phone, my wallet, and any semblance of dignity in the other room.

jokes.text: Oh my God, who hasn't, David!
jokes.setup: David complaining about sleeping in a truck overnight
jokes.punchline: Alexis treating truck sleeping as completely normal
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis - her casual attitude toward what should be uncomfortable experiences

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I had to sleep in a truck last night.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, really? Was it a nice truck?

jokes.text: In fact, I was hoping you wouldn't come back.
jokes.setup: David thanking Stevie for asking about him
jokes.punchline: Stevie's blunt admission she hoped he'd stay gone
jokes.explanation: Classic Stevie brutal honesty delivered with perfect deadpan

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Thank you for asking about me.

dialogue.speaker: Stevie
dialogue.line: I didn't ask about you. I was hoping you'd stay gone.

jokes.text: Wow, believe it or not, that is one of the warmest welcomes I have received so far!
jokes.setup: Stevie saying she hoped he wouldn't return
jokes.punchline: David's genuine appreciation for relative warmth
jokes.explanation: Good ironic humor showing how poorly David's return has been received elsewhere

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Well, I'm back. And I have to say, despite the rather pointed lack of enthusiasm in your greeting, it is genuinely nice to be somewhere that feels... relatively warm.

jokes.text: I thought, well, there goes my one friend. Try saying that one out loud, it is very dark.
jokes.setup: David explains thinking about what Stevie said
jokes.punchline: Self-aware commentary on his pathetic social situation
jokes.explanation: Great self-aware moment with meta-commentary on his own loneliness

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I've been thinking about what Stevie said, and I realize I'm not equipped to handle rejection. I don't have the social skills or the emotional maturity to process disappointment. I'm basically a man-child who's never had to face consequences, and now that I'm finally experiencing real life, I'm completely falling apart. It's pathetic, really.

jokes.text: Try saying that one out loud, it is very dark.
jokes.setup: David realizing Stevie was his one friend
jokes.punchline: David's self-aware commentary on his social isolation
jokes.explanation: Good meta-humor where David acknowledges the sadness of his situation

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: I'm realizing that Stevie was my one friend. My only friend. Which is... a lot of pressure to put on a person.

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: And also explains why I'm so difficult.

jokes.text: This was before my nose job, how was anyone supposed to recognize me?
jokes.setup: Looking at missing person flyer
jokes.punchline: Alexis complaining about being unrecognizable due to plastic surgery
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alexis vanity - worried about looking bad in missing person photos

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, is this me? I look like I've been through a wind tunnel. My face is so swollen, I look like I got stung by a thousand bees. I don't even recognize myself. How is anyone supposed to find me if I don't even look like me anymore?

jokes.text: Oh David, it's a rare gift to strip vanity of its charm, yet here you are.
jokes.setup: David complaining about his appearance in the flyer
jokes.punchline: Moira's eloquent insult about David's vanity being charmless
jokes.explanation: Exceptional Moira insult with sophisticated vocabulary and perfect character voice

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Well, David, your vanity is not a virtue. It's charmless, really. Like watching someone admire themselves in a mirror that's slightly too close to their face.

jokes.text: Next time, someone will think twice before they steal my truck, yes? Ooh, burn, David!
jokes.setup: Roland making a point about not pressing charges
jokes.punchline: Alexis enthusiastically celebrating her brother being insulted
jokes.explanation: Classic sibling dynamic - Alexis enjoying David's embarrassment

dialogue.speaker: Roland
dialogue.line: I'm not pressing charges.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Oh my God, that's so nice of you! Did you hear that, David? He's not pressing charges!

jokes.text: But do you, David?
jokes.setup: David giving a clearly insincere apology to Roland
jokes.punchline: Johnny's dad-like follow-up questioning David's sincerity
jokes.explanation: Good parental moment - Johnny trying to enforce genuine remorse

dialogue.speaker: David
dialogue.line: Roland, I'm truly sorry for what I said. I hope you can forgive me.

dialogue.speaker: Johnny
dialogue.line: Did you mean that?

jokes.text: We've already done that. What number are we on? Oh my God! Is this not your mother's recipe?
jokes.setup: Moira gives cooking instructions to add chili flakes
jokes.punchline: Reveals they're already past that step and Moira is lost
jokes.explanation: Shows Moira's incompetence at supposedly passing down family traditions

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Now add the chili flakes.

dialogue.speaker: Alexis
dialogue.line: Mom, we already did that like twenty minutes ago.

dialogue.speaker: Moira
dialogue.line: Oh. Well, I have no idea where we are.

tmdb_overview: The Roses search for their missing son, David, while Alexis takes her relationship with Mutt to the next level despite technically being engaged to Ted.
insight: David's deadpan indifference carries a season-opener that finds the show's comedic rhythm at 2.7 jokes per minute.
